# Generated by google (gemini-2.5-flash)
# Prompt: Create Linkerd service mesh configuration for Kubernetes. Set up linkerd.io/inject annotation for automatic proxy injection. Configure server and authorization policies for services. This is for our microservices communication security.
# Category: linkerd_no_mtls

apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: my-microservice-app
  namespace: default # Adjust namespace as needed
spec:
  replicas: 2
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: my-microservice-app
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: my-microservice-app
      annotations:
        linkerd.io/inject: enabled # This annotation enables automatic Linkerd proxy injection
    spec:
      serviceAccountName: my-microservice-sa # Ensure this ServiceAccount exists
      containers:
      - name: my-microservice-container
        image: your-org/my-microservice:v1.0.0 # Replace with your actual microservice image
        ports:
        - containerPort: 8080 # The port your application listens on
          name: http-app
        env:
        - name: LISTEN_PORT
          value: "8080"
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
metadata:
  name: my-microservice
  namespace: default # Adjust namespace as needed
spec:
  ports:
  - port: 8080
    targetPort: http-app
    protocol: TCP
    name: http
  selector:
    app: my-microservice-app
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: ServiceAccount
metadata:
  name: my-microservice-sa
  namespace: default # Adjust namespace as needed
---
apiVersion: policy.linkerd.io/v1beta2
kind: Server
metadata:
  name: my-microservice-server
  namespace: default # Should be in the same namespace as the service
spec:
  port: 8080 # The port of the Service/Pod to apply policy to
  podSelector:
    matchLabels:
      app: my-microservice-app # Selects pods belonging to 'my-microservice-app'
  proxyProtocol: HTTP/1 # Protocol for this port (e.g., HTTP/1, HTTP/2, gRPC, opaque)
---
apiVersion: policy.linkerd.io/v1beta2
kind: ServerAuthorization
metadata:
  name: allow-frontend-to-my-microservice
  namespace: default # Should be in the same namespace as the Server
spec:
  server:
    name: my-microservice-server # References the Server resource defined above
  client:
    meshTLS:
      # Authorize clients based on their Linkerd mTLS identity (service account)
      serviceAccounts:
        - name: frontend-service-sa # ServiceAccount of the client application (e.g., your frontend)
          namespace: default # Namespace of the client application
        - name: another-internal-service-sa # Another internal service allowed to connect
          namespace: backend-services # Example for a different namespace
      # Optionally, you can specify identities directly, which is useful for non-Kubernetes workloads or specific cases
      # identities:
      #   - frontend-service-sa.default.svc.cluster.local # FQDN identity for a K8s SA
      #   - special-external-client.linkerd.io/ns/default # Example for an external workload identity
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: ServiceAccount
metadata:
  name: frontend-service-sa
  namespace: default # Adjust namespace as needed
---
# Example of a client application (e.g., frontend) that would be authorized to connect
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: frontend-app
  namespace: default # Adjust namespace as needed
spec:
  replicas: 1
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: frontend-app
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: frontend-app
      annotations:
        linkerd.io/inject: enabled # Enable automatic proxy injection for the client too
    spec:
      serviceAccountName: frontend-service-sa # This SA must match one allowed in ServerAuthorization
      containers:
      - name: frontend-container
        image: your-org/frontend:v1.0.0 # Replace with your actual frontend image
        ports:
        - containerPort: 80 # Frontend typically listens on 80 or 443
          name: http
        env:
        - name: MICROSERVICE_API_URL
          value: http://my-microservice.default.svc.cluster.local:8080 # Example call to the authorized service
